observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the:

MSWD   Officer and/or Personnel In-Charge for the distribution of relief assistance to attach the distribution lists and acknowledgement by beneficiaries to the disbursement vouchers, and,

BAC  Secretariat  to furnish the Auditor concerned copies of the aforementioned postings to said website or equivalent postings.
observations.reason: No documents were submitted evidencing compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to issue an Executive Order or equivalent directive to create an Inventory Committee to:
Conduct the annual physical count of all PPEs in order to update the RPCPPE, otherwise, conduct inventory per account, targeting the most visible ones like buildings and structures heavy equipment and motor vehicles, starting from the current year acquisitions and backward, a manner to ease the inventory taking for manpower and time constraints;
Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and,
Maintain the prescribed stock cards/property cards and (SLC)/(PPELC), and reconcile it with the RPCPPE, together with the Municipal Accountant, to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to notify payees with stale checks amounting to ₱167,155.55 in the Bank Reconciliation Statements that are long outstanding for cancellation and replacement, if warranted, and be adjusted in the books with their complete supporting documents to present a more reliable Cash in Bank balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact and in full daily or the next banking day to the authorized government depository bank consonant to Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to update and provide the necessary and relevant information of the unexpended balance of the continuing appropriation of the LDRRMF for the disclosure to the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the LDRRMO to formulate, prepare and implement the LDRRM Plan in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framework and policies on disaster risk reduction and ensure that the programs, projects and activities have  direct linkages with the four thematic areas of LDRRMF; and
Direct the former and the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging disbursements not related to the four thematic areas of LDRRMF.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to reconcile and verify with the Provincial Accountant the records of the subsidiary accounts with unidentified source agencies.
Acting Municipal Treasurer to assist in the inventory of the pertinent Memoranda of Agreement and reconcile fund transfers with the accounting records to determine their source agencies. For idle funds with immaterial amounts, refund the unused balance to the source agency or seek written approval from the source agency so that the unused balance may be utilized for certain purposes.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the correcting journal entry to reclassify the said accounts with the premise that the books were already closed, as follows:
Other Structures                     (1 07 04 990)              ₱459,283.00
Government Equity                (3-01-01-010)                                       ₱459,283.00
Eventually, due to its fund source, the same would be a reconciling item under the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts at year-end.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to revisit the formerly created Inventory Committee and if warranted, issue an Executive Order to re-create a new one to:

 Maintain the prescribed stock cards/property cards and (SLC)/(PPELC), and reconcile it with the RPCPPE, together with the Municipal Accountant, to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements.

Conduct the annual physical count of all PPEs in order to update the RPCPPE, otherwise, conduct inventory per account, targeting the most visible ones like buildings and structures heavy equipment and motor vehicles, starting from the current year acquisitions and backward, a manner to ease the inventory taking for manpower and time constraints.

Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.
We further recommended the Local Chief Executive to assign competent personnel to facilitate the setting up of clear policies in the operation, supervision, and monitoring of the Inventory Committee to ensure compliance with the foregoing guidelines.
observations.reason: No inventory committee nor enough number of personnel to maintain such records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant:

To ensure that cash advances be liquidated within the reglementary period or as soon as the purposes for which they were drawn had been served.

To require the accountable officers to adhere with the rules and regulations on liquidation of cash advances as provided in the foregoing Presidential Decree No.1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.

That for those accountable officers who have liquidated their cash advances but still have remaining balances, demand should be made for the refund of the remaining amounts.

To issue demand letters to the aforementioned accountable officers.
observations.reason: Unliquidated accounts pertain to those under the SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to make timely requests of snapshots from the bank and also copies of debit/credit memos, in order to prepare and submit bank reconciliation statements within the reglementary period consonant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011. 

Also, we recommended that the Municipality enrol for We access account with the Landbank of the Philippines for accessibility of viewing records.

If fund warrants, we further recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to consider reinforcing or training other staff/personnel of the Accounting Office for appropriate technical skills/knowledge in order to assist in the preparation of bank reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: Non-receipt of bank debit/credit memos had hampered the timely reconciliation of related accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct all personnel concerned to maximize the utilization of the 20% Municipal Development Fund allocated for implementation of various projects, programs, and activities to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.reason: There were still balances of partially implemented 20% DF the Municipality as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the MDRRMO to adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Municipal Development Council. Relative thereto, the Municipal Accountant is advised to prepare promptly the said report to the MDRRMO.
observations.reason: Submission of reports were not updated during the pandemic period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to update and provide the necessary and relevant information of the unexpended balance of the continuing appropriation of the LDRRMF for the disclosure to the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: Only general information was provided in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the:

Supply Officer to prepare the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) for all the supplies/goods distributed during the pandemic and  determine the undistributed /unconsumed items for recording of the inventory.

Municipal Accountant to take up inventory account as provided by the Government Accounting Manual and make necessary adjustments on the undistributed /unconsumed items, if any.
observations.reason: Preparation of pertinent report and forms was hampered due to lack of personnel in the Supply Section of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to attach the written confirmation by HOPE indicating the existence and veracity of the grounds relied upon on the foregoing procurements, pursuant to Item 3.3 of the GPPB Circular No. 04-2016 dated December 20, 2016, as basis and justification for Negotiated Procurement - Emergency Cases modality by the Bids and Awards Committee.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson and Members of the Bids and Awards Committee to facilitate the submission of the lacking documentary requirements, namely, (1) request for quotation (RFQ) or request for proposal (RFP), (2) mayor’s or business permit, and (3) omnibus sworn statement pursuant to Item 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of the same circulars. Further, the Municipal Mayor, Bids and Awards Committee and the End-User Unit (OIC-Municipal Health Officer) should always consider the underlying reason to support the Negotiated Procurement - Emergency Cases modality pursuant to Item 3.4 of the same circular.
observations.reason: Complete documents were not submitted evidencing full compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:

Municipal Accountant to identify the composition of the unutilized balance of the fund transfer from other sources which were not stipulated. 


OIC-Municipal Treasurer to refund the unused balance to the source agency or seek written approval from the source agency so that the unused balance may be utilized for certain purposes. 
Concerned Department Heads to review the corresponding Memoranda of Agreement of the said fund transfers and implement the projects as soon as possible.
observations.reason: Some balances were not yet identified as to their sources.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of the documents evidencing settlements thereof, such as, official receipts, disbursement vouchers and/or payrolls related to the payment/refund and corresponding journal entry vouchers as references for the issuance of NSSDC by this Commission.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned personnel/official to exert effort on the compliance of the full settlement of audit disallowances pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.reason: Copies of payroll/DVs/ORs corresponding to the settlement of the said disallowances were not yet consolidated and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the members of the Local Finance Committee which is composed of the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer, to recommend the appropriate tax and other revenue measures which may be appropriate to support the revision of the Market Revenue Code.

Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to issue revised lease contract to the market stall holders effecting the revised Municipal Tax Ordinance No. 01 series of 2009 dated February 24, 2009.

Request the Sangguniang Bayan to effect the revision of the Municipal Market Code to attain realistic rates of taxes/fees and enhance collections.
observations.reason: Related revenue plans had been suspended in the light of the recent calamities and pandemic which greatly affected the economy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis of setting up the RPT/SET Receivable as prescribed by the manual. And for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments so that the RPT/SET Receivable are of the same amount and also equal to Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Tax Income.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

LDRRM Officer II to attach course syllabus/program of lectures on every training/seminar/workshop in conformity with Item 5.7.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. 
LDRRM Officer II to obtain the service of lecturers/trainers who are competent in their field of expertise to handle trainings relative to DRRM.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the above-mentioned lacking documents pursuant to  Section 4 (6) of P.D No. 1445.
observations.reason: No recent documents submitted evidencing full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance/strategize collection efforts by conducting intensive tax campaign and sending out personnel to the barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time so that RPT and SET receivables are realized and tax delinquencies are collected.
observations.reason: Recent natural calamities and the pandemic had hampered the said tax collection efforts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to trace the records of the accounts which have negative balances and prepare necessary adjusting entries under the Due to NGAs account. Moreover, it is recommended to reclassify the ₱303,612.31 from Due to LGUs to Due to NGAs and review in details any fund which was possibly inappropriately recorded. 

b. ICO-Municipal Treasurer to assist in the inventory of the pertinent Memoranda of Agreement and reconcile fund transfers with the accounting records to determine their source agencies. For idle funds with immaterial amounts, refund the unused balance to the source agency or seek written approval from the source agency so that the unused balance may be utilized for certain purposes.
observations.reason: Both departments had been coordinating for the reconciliation of Fund Transfer balances, however, there were those accounts which still need references or documents for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Chairperson direct the Members of Local Council for Protection of Children (LCPC) to prepare and submit the Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC) and Children’s Profile.

We further recommended that the LCPC adhere to the pertinent provisions of Item II .1 of DILG- MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure strengthening and implementation of programs, projects, and activities (PPAs).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Chairperson and Members of the LSB formulate a three-year program to be integrated in the LGU’s CDP and LDIP prior to the formulation and preparation of its Annual Budget for the incoming year.

We further recommended that the ICO - Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant submit to the LSB certified statement containing the following: 1) actual SEF income for the immediately preceding fiscal year; (2) actual SEF income for the first two quarters of the current year; (3) the estimated SEF income for the last two quarters of the current year; and (4) the annual estimates for the ensuing year not later than July 15 of the current year.

We finally recommended that the LSB submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF Utilization, as prepared by the Municipal Accountant to the DepEd CO through appropriate channels, copy furnished Local Sanggunian, DBM Regional Offices and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter for quarterly reports and not later than February 15of the following year for annual reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Chairperson of the LDRRM Council direct the LDRRM Officer II to comply with the minimum standards for equipage which should be purchased/owned and be maintained by the LDRRM Office of the Municipality. Proper inventory taking and recording should also be conducted annually to ensure their proper condition, safekeeping, and accountability thereof.

We further recommended that the LDRRM Council through LDRRM Officer II in coordination with MPDO and MEO design and program a permanent and disaster resilient evacuation center (multipurpose center) to be included in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and LDRRM Plan consistent with the minimum standards set by the NDRRM Council.

We also recommended that the LDRRM Council through LDRRM Officer II coordinate with MSWD, MHO and other concerned offices in complying with requirements/requisites listed in the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards, Volume II.

We finally recommended that the LDRRM Council observe adherence to the other five categories of disaster preparedness minimum standard namely structures, systems and policies, plans, data requirements and trainings.
observations.reason: LGU still need additional equipage, system and policies and trainings to foster efficient and effective LDRRM operations.
